e2b.intro_desc: E2B provides Agent with an isolated, secure, and reproducible cloud sandbox runtime. After it is enabled, the system can execute code, run commands, process files, or use toolchains inside an isolated sandbox without directly affecting the main service environment, while also making it easier to spin up consistent runtimes from templates.
e2b.feature_isolation_desc: Each task runs in its own Sandbox to reduce environment risks caused by command execution and file handling.
e2b.feature_template_desc: Use Template to specify base images or environment templates so Agent gets stable and consistent runtime dependencies.
e2b.feature_command_desc: Configure timeout and execution user to better control task duration, permission boundaries, and resource usage.
e2b.switch_desc: You must fill in and validate the connection parameters before E2B can be enabled. Assistant
e2b.preview_empty: E2B is currently disabled. Connection parameters will be shown here after it is enabled.
e2b.msg_load_failed: Failed to load E2B settings. Please try again later.
e2b.msg_save_failed: Failed to save E2B settings. Please try again later.

help.api_key: Used to authenticate access to the E2B service.
help.api_base_url: The E2B API service URL. A full URL is required.
help.sandbox_domain: The domain used when accessing the sandbox externally.
help.template: The environment template used when creating the Sandbox.
help.timeout: The lifecycle timeout for the Sandbox.
help.command_timeout: The timeout for a single command execution.
help.command_user: The system user used when commands run inside the Sandbox.

e2b.validator_url: Please enter a valid http or https URL
e2b.validator_positive_integer: {label} must be an integer greater than 0
